title: Book Reading Time Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Long Does It Take to Read a Book?
description: Calculate how long it takes to read any book based on pages, daily reading minutes, and genre. Predicts completion date · Free & instant.
seoKeywords: how long to read a book
how many days to finish a book
intro: How long does it take to read a book? It's one of the most searched questions by readers. A 400-page book takes about 2 weeks at 30 minutes daily, or just 3 days at 3 hours daily. This calculator predicts your completion date based on page count, daily reading minutes, and reading speed adjusted by genre. Perfect for planning your annual reading list, checking if a book club commitment is realistic, or estimating time for required academic reading.
keyTakeaway: A **300-page book** at **30 min/day** takes **~12–18 days** depending on genre. For dense classics, double the time.
useCases: Build a realistic annual reading list.
Check if you can keep up with a weekly book club.
Plan your semester's required reading schedule.
See if a thick book fits in your calendar this month.
explanation:
## Formula

```
days = (pages × words_per_page) / (wpm × min_per_day)
```

## Reading Time Table: 300 Pages (30 min/day, 250 WPM)

| Genre | Days |
|---|---|
| Thriller/Romance | 9 |
| General Fiction | 11 |
| Essay/Non-fiction | 13 |
| Classic/Literary | 16 |
| Academic/Philosophy | 22 |

## Average Adult Reading per Year

- **Casual**: 3–5 books.
- **Regular**: 12–20 books.
- **Avid**: 30–50 books.
- **Extreme**: 100+ books.

Bill Gates reads ~50 books/year. Warren Buffett reads 80%+ of his day.

## Tips to Increase Your Reading Time

1. **Audiobooks during commute**: 1h/day = 300h/year = 30–40 books.
2. **Morning pages**: 15 min before breakfast.
3. **Before bed**: 20 min screen-free reading.
4. **Weekend marathons**: rack up 2–3 hours Saturday/Sunday.

## Realistic Annual Reading Goals

For 20 books/year, aim for ~30 min/day. For 50 books, ~75 min/day + audiobooks. For 100+, reading is your primary hobby.

Also check [reading time by page count](/reading-time-by-page-count) and [reading time by book genre](/reading-time-by-book-genre).

faq.q: How many books per year should I read?
faq.a: The average American adult reads 12 books/year (Pew 2021). Regular readers aim for 20+. About 30% of adults don't read any books annually. The 'right' number is personal—focus on consistency over quantity.

Yes, for practical purposes. Recent research shows retention and comprehension are comparable to print reading, though attention span can vary. Many readers combine print + audiobooks to maximize their annual total.

faq.q: Should I finish a book I'm not enjoying?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Try the 50-page rule (Nancy Pearl): if you're over 50, read the first 50 pages; if younger, subtract your age from 50 and read that many pages before deciding.

faq.q: How can I read more books?
faq.a: Add audiobooks to your commute (easily +30–40 books/year). Read 15 min before breakfast, 20 min before bed, and dedicate 2–3 hours on weekends. Most readers find 30–45 min/day sustainable.
Adult average is 200–250 words per minute (WPM). Light fiction: ~250 WPM. Literary fiction: ~200–220 WPM. Academic/philosophy: ~150 WPM. Audiobooks at 1× speed: ~150 WPM equivalent.

faq.q: Can you read multiple books at once?
faq.a: Yes, and many readers do. Try pairing fiction + non-fiction (they don't compete mentally). Some readers use different books for different times of day (e.g., thriller before bed, essays in the morning).

Enter your book's page count, daily reading minutes, and genre. The calculator multiplies pages × words-per-page, divides by your reading speed (WPM adjusted for genre), then by daily minutes. Everything runs instantly in your browser—no data is sent to a server.

The results are estimates based on average reading speeds by genre. Accuracy depends on how your reading speed matches the averages. Use it as a reference, not a guarantee—personal reading habits vary widely.

faq.q: Is this calculator free? Do I need an account?
faq.a: Yes, 100% free with no account required. We don't collect email, personal data, or require registration. Use it unlimited times at no cost.
